sections.paragraph: an unused root, having, as I consider, the same meaning as Syriac ܟܚܰ to pant, Germ. keuchen (compare the roots of similar sound, which also are onomatopoetic, נָפַח, אָנַח, נָהַג); hence, to exert one’s strength, whence כֹּחַ strength, power. I consider the Arab. كاح to overcome in battle, as a secondary root, formed from the Hebr. כֹּחַ.
